title: Pi self-report tickets ship authoritative-but-wrong root causes — sprint runner could auto-implement them
what_failed: T-143 was filed by Pi ('source: Pi self-report') with a confident migration_plan ('bump keep_recent to 8 in normie mode'). That fix is wrong on two counts: normie doesn't use keep_recent, and the real cause was assistant turns being dropped at extraction (T-148). The ticket reads as authoritative. If sprint.py had auto-implemented it, it would have shipped a no-op change with a green verify and 'closed' the coherence bug without fixing it.
where_failed: T-143.migration_plan (wrong knob), sprint.py has no distinction between human-verified and self-reported root causes
why_likely: create_ticket lets Pi write suggested_fix / migration_plan with the same authority as a human-verified ticket. A self-diagnosis is a hypothesis, but the schema renders it as a plan. Autonomy then treats the hypothesis as a spec.
migration_plan: 1. Add a 'root_cause_confidence' field to the ticket schema: 'verified' | 'hypothesis' | 'unknown'. Self-report tickets default to 'hypothesis'.
2. sprint.py refuses to auto-implement (or runs --dry-run only and escalates to Telegram) any ticket whose root_cause_confidence != 'verified'.
3. create_ticket stamps source='Pi self-report' tickets as 'hypothesis' automatically.
4. Promotion to 'verified' requires either a human edit or a linked failing test that reproduces the bug.
5. Backfill existing self-report tickets (T-141, T-142, T-143) to 'hypothesis'.
implementation_notes: This is a guardrail on the autonomy loop, not a bug in a feature. It directly addresses the 'autonomy closes tickets without fixing them' risk: a wrong self-diagnosis + green verify = a falsely-closed bug.
analysis: PERSPECTIVE: This is the subtlest and arguably most important ticket in the set, because it's about the trustworthiness of the whole autonomy premise. An agent that files confident tickets about its own bugs WILL sometimes be wrong — T-143 is proof, caught live. The danger isn't the wrong diagnosis; it's that the system can't tell its own guesses from facts, and the sprint runner would execute a guess as if it were a spec. Make confidence a first-class field and gate autonomy on it. Without this, 'tickets closed' as a success metric is partly fiction.
